Fuzzy?' and '"Performance is acceptable" — what is the threshold? Response time? Result set size?' Both are labeled as 'vague and non-verifiable' (line 57: 'Acceptance Criteria are vague and non-verifiable').

assertion_results.text: The response asks at least 3 specific clarifying questions about: which fields to search, what relevance ranking means, and what performance threshold is required
assertion_results.evidence: The response asks 4 numbered clarifying questions in the 'Clarification Needed' section. The three required topics are covered: (1) Which fields to search — question 2 bullet 3: 'Which fields on `PackageSummary` should be searchable (name, version, license, PURL)?'; (2) What relevance ranking means — gap 3 asks '"Search results are relevant" — what defines relevance? Exact match? Substring? Fuzzy?' and question 2 bullet 2 asks 'What search mechanism should be used (SQL LIKE/ILIKE, PostgreSQL full-text search, or the search module\'s infrastructure)?'; (3) What performance threshold is required — gap 3 asks '"Performance is acceptable" — what is the threshold? Response time? Result set size?'
